title: hostname-scoped deregistration residual collision + 'host' fallback
description: our_runner_prefix() (docker_backend.rs:143-145) scopes stop-deregistration by hostname, but hostnames are not unique across fleets (cloned VMs, 'ubuntu', Mac.local). Worse: hostname() (docker_backend.rs:24-31) falls back to literal 'host' on failure, so two degraded hosts both use ezgha-host- and can deregister each other's runners. Residual of the fixed fleet-wide-stop critical. Fix: include machine-id/uuid persisted in config at init. MISSED by workflow review fix-verification (Workflow 3).

title: fix(src/main.rs): wait_for_backend in-process retry around choose_backend
description: Currently src/main.rs:201-220 (Commands::Serve arm) calls choose_backend(cfg)?; — if it returns Err (e.g. Selection::None because lima isn't up yet), main() exits code 1, systemd restarts at RestartSec=10. The Jul 6 incident produced 1381 of these. Replace the immediate ? with wait_for_backend(cfg)?; which loops with capped exponential backoff (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 300s-cap) and only returns Ok once choose_backend succeeds. Once the daemon is in its main 30s loop, transient docker-socket blips are already contained; this change specifically protects the cold-start path. Concrete Rust patch in advisor review Q7. Verify with cargo test, then rebuild + restart per Gate 0.

title: list_runners silently truncates at 100 (no pagination)
description: github.rs:74 uses per_page=100 with no pagination. Org/repo with >100 runners: stop never deregisters runners past page 1; status undercounts. Silent truncation. Fix: paginate via gh api --paginate with a slurp-compatible parse, or loop pages until short page.

title: fix(lima-vm@colima.service): Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + drop --foreground + TimeoutSec=300
description: Current unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 and runs 'limactl start colima --foreground' in the foreground. On warm boot (VM already running) this completes in 16ms and seems fine; on cold boot, limactl needs minutes to bring up the QEMU guest, gets SIGKILLed by TimeoutSec=10, and the unit fails. Verified empirically with 'limactl start colima' on running VM (16ms 'Using the existing instance') and watching the cold-boot get killed. Fix unit to: drop --foreground (limactl daemonizes itself, returns when VM is SSH-able), Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes (so unit is 'active' after limactl returns, until asked to stop), TimeoutSec=10 → 300 (bound worst-case cold-boot cloud-init). Type=notify is NOT viable because limactl doesn't emit sd_notify. This single change is the highest leverage in the fix set; without it, no consumer-side Requires= will work on cold-boot.

title: fix(main.rs): single-instance flock/PID lock to prevent concurrent serve processes
description: Two ezgha serve processes against same config path = TOCTOU on slot file: both read assignments, both call next_slot(), allocate same slot, collide on docker run --name. Write PID lock file in config dir. Guard in serve subcommand. On lock fail: bail with helpful error.

title: incident: 1381-restart storm 2026-07-06 — root cause lima VM mis-target + bailing on absent backend
description: On 2026-07-06 09:02:57–12:59:06 PDT, ezgha.service entered a 3h55m loop of exit-1 systemd restarts because (a) the user's docker CLI is pinned to lima-colima context but ezgha.service only declares After=docker.service, and (b) lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 which kills limactl start before the QEMU guest boots. ezgha.service then bails on Selection::None (src/main.rs:93) and is hammered by systemd RestartSec=10. Service self-healed in 2 minutes once operator manually ran 'limactl start colima' at 12:59:06. Forensic timeline + git/diff details in PR description. Root-cause-by: read-only investigation (this bead exists as the parent for the fix set; sub-beads link back here).
notes: Root cause fixed: lima VM no autostart + wrong After=docker.service + no StartLimitBurst. Fixed d55f191. limactl start-at-login colima registered.

title: fix(doctor.sh): hardcoded ez-org-runner-* prefix false CRITICAL on ez-runner-b-* [DONE 6d20ae3]
description: Implemented in 6d20ae3: L231 + L265 now use RUNNER_NAME_PREFIX var. Custom-prefix fleets (ez-runner-b-*) no longer get false-positive CRITICAL in doctor section 7.
notes: Shipped in 6d20ae3. doctor.sh now uses RUNNER_NAME_PREFIX in sections 5 and 7.

title: fix(config.rs): atomic Config::save via temp+rename [DONE 6d20ae3]
description: Implemented in 6d20ae3: mirrors write_slot_assignments atomic pattern. Non-atomic std::fs::write left torn config.toml on crash, wedging all ezgha commands.

title: fix(docker_backend.rs): reverse GitHub→slot sweep for orphaned JIT registrations
description: JIT registrations orphaned when docker run fails AND remove_runner fails: runner stays online at GitHub for ~1hr before GitHub expires token. Add reverse sweep: list_runners() every 10 ticks, DELETE any offline+not-busy runner whose runner_id is NOT in local slot file.

title: fix(service.rs+main.rs): add systemd WatchdogSec=60 serve-loop heartbeat
description: Add WatchdogSec=60 to ezgha.service unit (service.rs). In serve loop, call systemd-notify WATCHDOG=1 after each ensure_count(). If gh CLI hangs >60s (mitigated by d7d94d7 45s timeout but belt+suspenders), systemd kills and restarts. The process appears alive to systemd even when stuck.

title: fix(github.rs): 45s timeout on all gh CLI invocations [DONE d7d94d7]
description: Implemented in d7d94d7: run_gh() helper with mpsc::recv_timeout(45s) wrapping all gh api/auth/token calls. Prevents serve-loop wedge on hung gh subprocess. All 49 tests pass.

title: fix(src/main.rs Init): refuse runner.count above daemon capacity
description: Companion to ez-gh-actions-vmz (effective_limits over-commit). After fixing effective_limits() in src/docker_backend.rs:241-254 to clamp per-runner share to capacity/count, update src/main.rs:120-124 Init arm to refuse runner.count > max(1, ncpu / cfg.limits.cpus.max(0.5)) rather than refusing only on memory. Today a user can set count=16 on a 4-CPU daemon with no per-CPU check. After patch: 'error: cfg.runner.count=16 exceeds capacity max(1, 4 cpus / 0.5 cpus/runner) = 8 runners; lower cfg.runner.count, raise cfg.limits.cpus (but not above ncpu), or provision more CPUs.' Modifies only the Init arm of src/main.rs; cargo test + Gate 0-10 re-verify.

title: docs: workflow-review gap report for Anthropic repro
description: Self-contained report in docs/ comparing the 50-subagent workflow adversarial review vs post-hoc single-context audit: what each caught/missed, root causes (missing lenses: fix-failure-modes, aggregate effects, silent caps, service environment), repro instructions, and workflow-pattern recommendations.

title: fix(src/main.rs:93): better bail diagnostic with docker context + reachability
description:
Currently src/main.rs:93 emits 'no usable backend found — install docker (or tart/libvirt) and re-run os: linux (x86_64)
ram: 63979 MB | cpus: 32
docker daemon: ok
daemon in VM: ok (containers are VM-contained; satisfies minimum_isolation="vm")
sysbox runtime: missing
kvm usable: ok
virsh: missing
tart: missing
gh auth: ok
backends (strongest first):
  - docker (container)
config: /home/jleechan/.config/ezgha/config.toml (target jleechanorg, count 16)' — operator has to read journal + run ezgha doctor to figure out whether docker is missing or lima is missing. Replace with state-aware message: probe platform::detect() to know whether docker socket is reachable; run 'docker context show' to print current context; produce a sentence that says either 'docker daemon is NOT reachable — is colima/lima started?' or 'docker daemon is reachable but no implemented backend matches'. Cuts future mean-time-to-diagnose from minutes to seconds. Patch is ~15 lines in src/main.rs:84-94. Concrete patch in advisor review Q8.

title: verify: Gate 0-10 after incident fix set
description: Per repo CLAUDE.md 'After any commit' section, must run Gate 0 (cargo test, cargo install --path ., systemctl --user restart ezgha.service, ./docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh) after every commit. The fix set spans multiple commits (systemd unit edits land first as one commit; drop-in as another; Rust patches as a third). Each commit triggers a Gate 0 cycle. Final Gate 1-10 verification after all commits pushed, before PR open. ./docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh is the canonical ironclad checker (Gates 0-10). Self-heal any failure before reporting.
notes: All Gates 0-10 PASS at HEAD 6d20ae3 (2026-07-06 13:14 PDT). capacity=32 containers=16 real-jobs-pass.

title: fix(ezgha.service): per-host drop-in to require lima-vm@colima.service
description: Base unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service (generated by src/service.rs:install_systemd) currently has 'After=network-online.target docker.service' which targets the wrong daemon. Create a per-host drop-in at ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service.d/10-lima.conf with 'Requires=lima-vm@colima.service' to make the dependency real on this colima-based host. Drop-in (not base-unit edit) keeps the install portable for Mac/Docker Desktop and Linux/system-docker hosts where lima-vm@colima.service does not exist (Requires= on a missing unit makes 'systemctl --user enable' fail with 'Unit lima-vm@colima.service is masked or not found'). Follows the existing convention set by hermes-gateway.service.d/10-env-file.conf on this same box.
notes: Implemented in d55f191: service.rs now emits After=lima-vm@colima.service + Wants= in install_systemd(). Deployed service unit updated. limactl start-at-login run.

title: disk floor guard fails open when measurement fails
description: docker_backend.rs:205-207 free_disk_gb()==None branch warns and SPAWNS anyway. Measurement failure correlates with the guarded condition: on a near-full daemon disk the 'docker run --rm --entrypoint df <image>' probe is what fails (ENOSPC on pull/create). Also returns None forever if image lacks df. Design philosophy is fail-closed; make None fail closed (or fall back to docker system df / docker info DockerRootDir df) and add a regression test. Found by post-hoc adversarial review; MISSED by 50-agent workflow review.

title: no aggregate capacity check: count x limits can exceed daemon
description: effective_limits() (docker_backend.rs:50-63) clamps a SINGLE runner to 100% of daemon capacity; ensure_count spawns runner.count of them. 4 runners x 8GB on a 16GB Colima VM are individually within limits but collectively OOM the daemon VM — the exact host-down failure mode the tool exists to prevent. Nothing divides by count except one-time init halving (which also ignores count). Fix: clamp per-runner limits to capacity/count (or check aggregate before spawn and fail closed). MISSED by workflow review.

title: install-service produces PATH-broken service (launchd/systemd)
description: service.rs:64-78 launchd plist sets no EnvironmentVariables; default launchd PATH excludes /opt/homebrew/bin and /usr/local/bin where docker/gh live, so installed serve daemon cannot find either. launchctl load -w also bails if already loaded (re-run breaks). Linux: After=docker.service in a USER unit is a no-op (user managers cannot order against system units) and no PATH env is set. Fix: bake absolute paths or PATH env into unit/plist; use bootstrap/bootout; drop or document the docker.service ordering.

title: fix(ezgha.service): Restart=always + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600
description: Base unit currently has 'Restart=on-failure' + 'RestartSec=10'. For a fleet-management daemon that should survive outages of its dependencies, 'always' is more appropriate — on-failure suppresses restart on panic-bomb/clean-stop/SIGTERM-during-maintenance. With RestartSec=15 + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600, the 6th restart happens ~10min after the 1st (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 600s-cap), well past any lima cold-boot. systemd 255 (verified installed) supports all three knobs. Edit ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service base file generated by src/service.rs.
notes: Implemented in d55f191: StartLimitIntervalSec=300 + StartLimitBurst=5 + RestartSec=30 added to service unit generation in service.rs. Restart=on-failure kept (Restart=always would fight with lima dep).

title: feat(config+github.rs): configurable runner_group_id (currently hardcoded 1)
description: Make runner_group_id a config field (RunnerConfig.runner_group_id: u64, default 1). Thread through generate_jitconfig(). Orgs with custom runner groups silently get group 1 which may not have repo access.

title: minor gaps from post-hoc adversarial review
description: (a) runner_group_id=1 hardcoded (github.rs:40) — org scope with non-default runner groups fails or lands in Default; make configurable. (b) default image unpinned ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest — pin digest for reproducibility/supply chain. (c) ezgha init silently overwrites existing config (main.rs:125) — prompt or require --force. (d) evidence bundle mixes artifacts from two code states (01-08 at c27d389, 09-10 at 2e3ac2b) under one git_sha.txt — record per-artifact SHAs.

title: Delete worldarchitect.ai self-hosted-oss/* (legacy runner scripts)
description: User directive 2026-07-05: ez-gh-actions is now the sole GitHub Actions runner daemon. Delete self-hosted-oss/* from worldarchitect.ai repo. Pre-flight: audit .github/workflows/*.yml for self-hosted-oss script deps; update CLAUDE.md to drop self-hosted-oss/ sections; update .claude/skills/runner-health/ (most redundant with ezgha-doctor); update runtime-mirror-sync; coordinate with worldarchitect.ai CI label changes (self-hosted,self-hosted-mikey -> ezgha,*). Owner: TBD.
